channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Nicky Katt of 'Dazed and Confused,' 'Boston Public' dies: reports
item.description: Nicky Katt, who was known for his roles in "Dazed and Confused" and "Boiler Room," has died, according to multiple reports. He was 54 years old.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KREX 5

item.title: Understanding mutual funds: A beginner's guide to investing
item.description: Despite recent stock market slumps and fears in the investment world, it's never a bad time to learn about strategies to boost your investing experience.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: Many of Silicon Valley's most prominent tech leaders have embraced President Donald Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Kansas man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 97-year-old Colorado woman's death of hypothermia ruled a homicide
item.description: She had been intentionally left out in the cold "without any way to return home, get adequate shelter, or call for help," the autopsy report said.
item.dc:creator: Dara Korn, NewsNation

item.title: How a fight over proxy voting for new parents upended the US House
item.description: Anna Paulina Luna was at her Florida home in fall 2023, caring for her newborn son and turning over a question in her mind as a member of Congress: “How do I change this?”
item.dc:creator: LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about activist Mahmoud Khalil and his attorneys' plan to appeal his deportation ruling
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge has ruled that a Palestinian Columbia University graduate student who participated in protests against Israel can be deported. Mahmoud Khalil's attorneys said they will appeal Friday's ruling.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Augusta man attends 62 Masters in a row
item.description: For many people, they’ll wait their whole life to go to The Masters. For others, they’ve been going their whole life.
item.dc:creator: Karlton Clay, Fox 5

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge will decide whether to proceed with Menendez brothers resentencing hearing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A judge was set to decide Friday whether to proceed with resentencing hearings for Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of murdering their parents, in light of Los Angeles' new district attorney opposing their release after 30 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KRON4

item.title: Black Power Ranger actor disagrees with writer on his casting
item.description: Walter Emanual Jones, the actor who portrayed the Black Power Ranger in the "Mighty Morphin Power Rangers," responded to a statement made by one of the series' writers calling the casting of a Black actor in the role a "mistake.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: What we know about the New York City helicopter crash that killed 6 people
item.description: Six people were killed when a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Could Trump's tariffs slow emissions? Sure, experts say, but at great cost overall
item.description: President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs have stirred widespread anxiety about a severe economic downturn -- and curiosity, for some, about how it might affect the world’s warming climate.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: US Attorney Habba investigating New Jersey governor over immigration enforcement policy
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — The top federal prosecutor in New Jersey says she has launched an investigation into Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy and state Attorney General Matt Platkin over the state's directive to local law enforcement not to cooperate with federal agent conducting immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amateur golfer urinates in creek at Masters Tournament, gets round of applause
item.description: U.S. Amateur champion Jose Luis Ballester had to take a different kind of relief Thursday at the Masters, just not the kind found in the Rules of Golf.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stocks climb but the dollar sinks as Wall Street closes a chaotic week
item.description: U.S. stocks are climbing in shaky trading Friday as President Donald Trump’s trade war with China escalates further and U.S. households get more worried about it.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stocks climb but the dollar sinks as Wall Street closes a chaotic week
item.description: U.S. stocks are climbing in shaky trading Friday as President Donald Trump’s trade war with China escalates further and U.S. households get more worried about it.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, KRON4

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family from Spain, pilot killed in NYC sightseeing helicopter crash
item.description: Photos posted on the helicopter company’s website showed the couple and their children smiling as they boarded just before the flight took off.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WGN 9

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she will prevent the Trump administration from ordering hundreds of thousands of Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans with temporary legal status to leave the country later this month.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US stocks dive as euphoria on Wall Street reverts to fear about US-China trade war
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks dove Thursday and surrendered a chunk of their historic gains from the day before as President Donald Trump’s trade war continues to threaten the economy.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Annual White House Christmas ornament honors state dinner culinary diplomacy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The art of culinary diplomacy, practiced by presidents at White House state dinners for more than 150 years, is the theme of this year's official White House Christmas ornament.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crashes in New York City's Hudson River; 6 dead
item.description: A Bell 206 helicopter belonging to a tour company crashed in the water near Jersey City and Hoboken around 3:15 p.m., according to NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, Fox 5

item.title: A Los Angeles woman arrested in Russia has been freed in a prisoner swap. Here's what we know
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Moscow has freed a Russian American convicted of treason in exchange for a Russian German man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S. The prisoner swap was completed Thursday and Ksenia Karelina was "on a plane back home to the United States,” U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump reverses Biden rule restricting water usage for showerheads and other home appliances
item.description: President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: St. George parents arrested after toddler found alone outside
item.description: Two parents in St. George were taken into custody on Wednesday after their 2-year-old son was found outside of the home unattended.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: This federal rule helped clear air over America's most beloved parks. Trump's EPA wants to kill it
item.description: During a hike in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park in 1995, Don Barger climbed Chilhowee Mountain hoping to gaze across the valley below. All he saw was a wall of gray haze. Today, he said, he can see some 50 miles (80 kilometers) across that same valley to the Cumberland Mountains.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Students react to nearly 50 international students' visas being revoked, records being removed across Utah
item.description: International students at institutions across Utah and around the country are having their records terminated or their visas revoked. Here's what some students said about it.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
